title: a place to get stock price apis for free
author: crazylegend25
selftext:
every time I tried building an ai stock trader, the most annoying part is getting good stock data without being rate limited by providers or making my own scraper. anyways I made a lil thing that kinda gets data from all over the web and makes it an api, its pretty good at stock prices so if you guys wanted to try using it and let me know how it is id appreciate it.

its [ekkoe.vercel.app](http://ekkoe.vercel.app)

just make an account, request whatever stock api’s u want, and then use it, its all free.

oh yea, id also really like feedback, so like if you dm me on what you think ill give you pro access for 6 months free of charge (when I eventually make a pro version lol), literally just tell me what u think, what’s shitty, what’s interesting, and yea I gotchu.

comments.author: lostninjah
comments.body: alpha vantage has free apis! but also if you need something for just your own use, i've been using ibkr gateway

replies.author: crazylegend25
replies.body: for now i have no rate limits at all so u can call higher than alpha vantage

comments.author: klymaxx45
comments.body: What kind of data are you getting? Is it live feed?

replies.author: crazylegend25
replies.body: yup, i get it directly from websites on google

replies.author: klymaxx45
replies.body: Would be nice to see what kind of financial data you’re getting and the lag time

replies.author: crazylegend25
replies.body: just take a look, if u type in “apple price from yahoo finance” you get an api. to be honest, it can do ANYTHING on the web, like it can even get Trump’s latest tweet, the number of points Shai scored tonight, the weather in New york, but like i’m still working on making that better, but stock prices work best rn

comments.author: Either_Door_5500
comments.body:
Building your own scraper for financial data is an absolute nightmare because the underlying source layouts change constantly and you end up spending more time maintaining the data pipeline than working on your actual trading models. If you are building an AI trader, you also run into issues with data normalization since different sources format income statements and balance sheets completely differently, which throws off the LLMs reasoning.

I have been working on an api in this space - it helps with structured and LLM friendly SEC data for devs - especially for backtests, which might be useful for moving away from unstable scrapers so your AI pipeline gets clean, programmatic historical data directly.

Happy to share more if you want to take a look.
